report.summary: Researched and built 2 new learn guides: Liquid Staking Tokens (LSTs) covering $66.86B TVL ecosystem with Lido, Rocket Pool, stETH vs rETH mechanics, and DeFi composability strategies; and Crypto Prediction Markets covering $18B+ monthly volume across Polymarket and Kalshi, trading strategies, and 2026 regulatory landscape.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Topic 1 — Liquid Staking Tokens (LSTs) in 2026: Total liquid staking TVL exceeds $66.86B with $86.4B combined LST market cap. LSTs represent 31.56% of DeFi market (48.59% including restaking). Lido dominates with $19-38B TVL and 23-29% Ethereum staking market share (9.17M ETH staked). Rocket Pool holds ~$1.19B TVL with decentralized minipool model. Two LST models: stETH (rebase — balance grows daily) vs rETH (exchange rate — token appreciates). DeFi composability enables yield stacking (lending, LPing, restaking on top of base staking yield). Key risks: smart contract, depeg, slashing, composability risk compounding. Other protocols: Coinbase cbETH, Mantle mETH, Frax sfrxETH, Binance WBETH.

Topic 2 — Crypto Prediction Markets in 2026: Kalshi + Polymarket logged $18B+ monthly volume in Feb 2026, up from <$2B in Aug 2025 (10x growth). Kalshi valued ~$11B, Polymarket ~$8B, both exploring $20B fundraising rounds. Kalshi: CFTC-regulated, 60%+ market share, USD-based. Polymarket: blockchain (Polygon), USDC-based, received CFTC no-action letter Jan 2026 enabling US re-entry. Trading mechanic: YES/NO shares at $0.01-$1.00 reflecting probability. Regulatory landscape mixed: CFTC chairman withdrew restrictive rules, but Nevada (Feb 2026) and Arizona AG (March 2026) lawsuits against Kalshi. BETS OFF Act proposed to ban war/government outcome markets. California targeting insider trading in prediction markets. New entrants: Coinbase and traditional betting sites entering market.
Sources: CoinBureau, DefiLlama, BingX, Datawallet, BeInCrypto, CoinMarketCap, Fortune, TechBuzz, DL News, TRM Labs, CryptoTimes, Changelly, BullPerks, Speedrun Ethereum.

sections.title: Content Created
items: liquid-staking-tokens-guide-2026: ~3,200-word intermediate guide covering what liquid staking is, how it works (deposit → receive LST → earn rewards → use in DeFi), top protocols comparison table (Lido, Rocket Pool, Coinbase, Mantle, Frax with TVL, token, model, and APY data), stETH vs rETH rebase/exchange-rate mechanics, DeFi composability strategies (lending, LP, restaking), risks (smart contract, depeg, slashing, composability stacking), getting-started guide, and 6 FAQs.

crypto-prediction-markets-guide-2026: ~2,800-word intermediate guide covering what prediction markets are, how YES/NO share mechanics work, Polymarket vs Kalshi comparison table (8 attributes: type, blockchain, currency, regulation, market share, volume, KYC, categories), market categories, 5 trading strategies (edge-based, arbitrage, market making, sector rotation, hedging), 2026 regulatory landscape (CFTC no-action letter, state lawsuits, BETS OFF Act, insider trading), risks (counterparty, liquidity, regulatory, efficiency compression), getting-started steps for both platforms, and 6 FAQs.
Data file: src/data/pages/learn-20260401-v9.ts with full PageData entries for both pages (metadata, SEO config, TOC, FAQs, internal links)

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: LST page — Title: 'Liquid Staking Tokens Guide 2026' (35 chars). Target keyword: liquid staking tokens guide 2026. Schemas: Article + FAQPage (6 questions). Internal links to 4 related guides (restaking-eigenlayer, depin, smart-wallets, defai-automation). OG article type with publishedTime + Twitter summary_large_image.

Prediction Markets page — Title: 'Crypto Prediction Markets Guide 2026' (40 chars). Target keyword: crypto prediction markets guide 2026. Schemas: Article + FAQPage (6 questions). Internal links to 4 related pages (perpetual-dex-trading, intent-based-trading, smart-wallets, crypto-tax). OG article type with publishedTime + Twitter summary_large_image.
Both pages include publishedTime/modifiedTime, word counts in structured data, author attribution, and cross-linking to complementary guides.

sections.title: Infrastructure Changes
items: Created data file: src/data/pages/learn-20260401-v9.ts with PageData entries for both pages
Both slugs marked status: 'queued' for PUBLISH_QUEUE addition
No interactive components needed — both are static learn guides with comparison tables and FAQ sections
Both pages use server components with proper Metadata exports (no 'use client' directive)
